articles.headline: Intel Joins Elon Musk’s Terafab Chip Project for Tesla and xAI
articles.summary: Intel partnered with Elon Musk’s Terafab initiative to develop semiconductors for Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI, boosting Intel shares by 4.2% and signaling shifts in AI chip supply chains. This could diversify sourcing away from Asian manufacturers, impacting global trade and hardware costs for AI infrastructure. Businesses gain potential new options amid ongoing chip shortages.

articles.headline: Amazon’s AWS AI Revenue Run Rate Tops $15 Billion Amid Surging Demand
articles.summary: Amazon's cloud unit reported a $15B AI revenue run rate in Q1 2026, signaling hyperscaler AI spending is driving measurable growth and boosting chip revenue to over $20B annually. This underscores rising cloud and AI infrastructure costs for businesses, with Amazon's 2026 capex tied to committed AI projects. Enterprises face higher investments in AI compute amid Meta's projected $115-135B capex.

articles.headline: ASE plans to invest more than US$3.1bn in new fab
articles.summary: ASE Technology Holding plans to invest over US$3.15 billion in a new advanced chip testing facility in Kaohsiung to meet AI-driven customer demand, addressing hardware supply chain bottlenecks. Taiwanese firms like ASE and TSMC face pressure to resolve choke points in global AI supply chains, impacting business operational efficiency and costs.
